<!-- README generated by AI. -->

# Punch

Punch is a small Go CLI for working with the Sangoma time tracking API. It can
list and create activities, list timesheet entries, and bulk-create weekday
timesheet entries from the activities configured in YAML.

## Requirements

- Go 1.24.3 or newer
- Access to the tracking API
- An API token for the tracking API

## Configuration

By default, Punch reads `config.yaml` from the current directory. You can point
to a different file with `--config`.

```yaml
api_token: "<api token>"
tracking_url: "https://tracking.example.com"
project_id: "1"
activities:
  - id: 1282
    duration: 8
```

Configuration fields:

- `api_token`: bearer token used for API requests.
- `tracking_url`: base URL for the tracking API.
- `project_id`: project/customer ID used when listing and creating activities.
- `activities`: activity IDs and durations used by `time add`.

Each configured activity has:

- `id`: the activity ID from the tracking API.
- `duration`: the number of hours to log for that activity each workday.

For `time add`, the activity durations should add up to `8` hours total for a
normal workday. For example, this configuration creates two entries per
weekday: 2 hours for activity `1282` and 6 hours for activity `1301`.

```yaml
activities:
  - id: 1282
    duration: 2
  - id: 1301
    duration: 6
```

The entries are created in the same order they appear in the config, starting
at 09:00. With the example above, activity `1282` runs from 09:00 to 11:00 and
activity `1301` runs from 11:00 to 17:00.

Environment variables can override part of the YAML configuration:

- `TRACK_API_TOKEN` overrides `api_token`.
- `TRACK_URL` overrides `tracking_url`.

Keep real API tokens out of committed config files. Prefer using
`TRACK_API_TOKEN` for local or CI usage.

## Run Locally

Install dependencies:

```sh
go mod download
```

Run the CLI:

```sh
go run . --help
```

Build a local binary:

```sh
go build -o punch .
```

## Usage

All commands accept the global `--config` flag:

```sh
punch --config ./config.yaml <command>
```

### Activities

List activities for the configured project:

```sh
punch activities list
```

Filter activities by a search term:

```sh
punch activities list --limit support
punch activities list -l support
```

Add a new activity interactively:

```sh
punch activities add
```

The command prompts for a name and comment, then creates a billable, visible
activity under the configured `project_id`.

### Time

List timesheet entries:

```sh
punch time list
```

List timesheet entries in a date/time range:

```sh
punch time list --begin 2026-05-01T00:00:00 --end 2026-05-31T23:59:59
```

Change the API page size:

```sh
punch time list --size 50
punch time list -s 50
```

Create weekday timesheet entries from configured activities:

```sh
punch time add --begin 2026-05-11 --end 2026-05-15
```

`time add` currently expects `--begin` and `--end` as dates in `YYYY-MM-DD`
format. It skips Saturdays and Sundays, starts each workday at 09:00, and adds
each configured activity in order using its configured duration. Before writing
entries, it prints a preview and asks for confirmation.

## Development

Run tests:

```sh
go test ./...
```

Format code:

```sh
gofmt -w .
```
